description: Japan's largest pump manufacturer and a leading global supplier of semiconductor process equipment. Operates five segments: (1) Precision Machinery (~32% of revenue) — dry vacuum pumps, CMP (Chemical Mechanical Planarization) equipment, abatement systems, and plating equipment for semiconductor fabs; holds ~27% global CMP market share (#2 behind Applied Materials ~42%); (2) Building Service & Industrial (~28%) — standard pumps, chillers, blowers for buildings and factories; #1 domestic market share in Japan; (3) Energy (~24%) — Elliott-brand custom centrifugal compressors, steam turbines, cryogenic LNG pumps; >50% global market share in ethylene plant compressors; (4) Environmental Solutions (~10%) — waste incineration plants, water treatment O&M; (5) Infrastructure (~6%) — large drainage/sewage pumps for Japanese municipalities (1,000+ pump stations). Founded 1912, HQ Haneda, Ota-ku, Tokyo. ~21,000 employees, 111 group companies, 41 countries. TSE Prime Market. Conducted 5-for-1 stock split July 1, 2024. FY2025 revenue ¥958.3B (+10.6% YoY), first ¥1 trillion guidance for FY2026. Six consecutive record profit years. TSMC's most-recognized Japanese CMP supplier (9+ excellence awards).

majorSuppliers: Japanese cast iron / stainless steel foundries — pump casings, impellers (no single named dominant supplier; multiple regional foundries)

Precision sheet-metal fabricators (Kyoto/Osaka/Kanto regions) — CMP equipment enclosures and structural parts; CADDi Manufacturing platform used to expand supplier base during 2022 semiconductor demand surge
EagleBurgmann (Germany/Japan JV) / John Crane — mechanical seals for pumps (TC/TC faces, Viton elastomers)
Motor suppliers (likely Nidec, Mitsubishi Electric, Toshiba) — electric motors for industrial pumps (not officially named)
PLC / sensor suppliers (likely Omron, Mitsubishi Electric, Keyence) — electronic controls for pump systems (not officially named)

Note: Long-term supply contracts cover ~30% of supply agreements. ~1,240 major suppliers (796 domestic, 444 overseas) assessed via CSR questionnaire (FY2022). Core CMP components are manufactured in-house at Fujisawa plant.

precisionMachinery_semiconductor: TSMC (CMP-0017) — confirmed; Ebara received TSMC 'Excellent Performance in CMP Equipment' award 9+ consecutive times; most-recognized Japanese semiconductor equipment supplier at TSMC
Samsung Electronics (CMP-0018) — inferred; Ebara claims 80%+ of world's top 20 chipmakers as customers; Samsung is #1 by volume
SK Hynix (CMP-0020) — inferred from top-20 chipmaker claim
Micron Technology (CMP-0021) — inferred from top-20 chipmaker claim
Intel Corporation (CMP-0019) — inferred from top-20 chipmaker claim
Kioxia Holdings (CMP-0014) — inferred; Kioxia is Japan's major NAND fab and a primary user of CMP equipment
SMIC (CMP-0066) — China's largest foundry; China Precision Machinery revenue grew +44% YoY in FY2024, driven by Chinese fab expansion (pre/early-sanctions period)
YMTC (CMP-0093) — China's leading memory fab; recipient of Precision Machinery equipment prior to tightening of export controls; now restricted
SAMCO Inc. (CMP-0060) — Ebara vacuum pumps integrated as subsystems in SAMCO's compound semiconductor process equipment
energy_elliott: SATORP (Saudi Aramco / TotalEnergies JV) — 22 units (compressors, pumps, turbines) for AMIRAL petrochemical complex, Jubail, Saudi Arabia
Sempra Infrastructure / Port Arthur LNG (Texas) — cryogenic pumps, expanders, BOG compressors via Bechtel EPC
Global petrochemical operators (ExxonMobil Chemical, Shell, BASF, Dow — inferred from Elliott's >50% ethylene compressor market share; not individually named in public filings)
infrastructure_japan: Tokyo Metropolitan Government — flood-control pump stations
Japanese municipal governments — 1,000+ drainage pump stations nationwide
>80 waste incineration and treatment facilities operated under O&M contracts
buildingIndustrial: Japan National Stadium — low-GWP centrifugal chillers
Chubu Centrair International Airport — absorption chillers and cooling towers
Shibuya Station redevelopment project — chillers and HVAC systems
Commercial real estate, hospitals, factories across Japan

FY2025_ended_Dec2025.note: Sixth consecutive year of record profits. Reported February 13, 2026.

dividendHistory.note: FY2024 special dividend was a one-time distribution. 5-for-1 stock split effective July 1, 2024; all figures post-split adjusted.

forexSensitivity.FY2025_H1_FXheadwind: -¥1.2B from yen appreciation vs. prior year
forexSensitivity.note: A weaker yen is a tailwind; a stronger yen is a headwind. Exposure meaningful but offset by Japan-based costs.

marketPositions.dryVacuumPumps: Top 5 globally; >200,000 units shipped; 80%+ of world's top 20 chipmakers are customers
marketPositions.drainagePumps_Japan: Installed at 1,000+ drainage pump stations across Japan

keyAchievements: TSMC Excellent Performance in CMP Equipment award — 9+ consecutive times (most among Japanese suppliers)
Six consecutive years of record profits (FY2020–FY2025)
Eliminated all cross-shareholdings by June 2020 (zero strategic equity in other companies)
5-for-1 stock split July 2024 to improve retail accessibility
FY2024 special dividend ¥115/share (post-split) as profit-sharing measure
FY2026: first-ever ¥1 trillion revenue milestone in guidance

top10Shareholders: Ichigo Trust PTE Limited (Singapore, activist): ~10.29% (reduced to ~8.99% by Jul 2025)
shareholderStructure.governanceNote: Ichigo Trust (Singapore-based value/activist investor) is largest non-custodian holder at ~10%. Ebara holds zero cross-shareholdings in other companies as of June 2020.

CMP_equipment: Hwatsing Technology (CMP-0088) — emerging Chinese domestic alternative
dryVacuumPumps: Atlas Copco / Edwards Vacuum — global leader
standardPumps: Grundfos, Xylem, KSB, Flowserve, Wilo, Sulzer (all global)

keyRisks: China exposure: ~22% of revenue from China; geopolitical tensions and export control tightening could restrict semiconductor equipment sales to Chinese fabs (SMIC, YMTC)
Semiconductor cycle: Precision Machinery (32% of revenue) is cyclical; fab capex downturns would impact this high-margin segment
Elliott compressor concentration: >50% share in ethylene makes Elliott highly exposed to petrochemical capex cycles
Forex: 66% overseas revenue; yen appreciation reduces JPY-translated profits
Competition in CMP: Applied Materials has ~42% share and superior R&D resources; may widen lead in advanced nodes
Supply chain: Precision machined parts and castings face capacity constraints during demand surges (experienced 2022)
growthDrivers: AI chip demand: Leading fabs (TSMC, Samsung, SK Hynix) expanding for AI accelerators — all require CMP equipment and dry vacuum pumps
China fab buildout: Domestic Chinese fab expansion driving Precision Machinery growth (+44% YoY in FY2024; subject to export control changes)
LNG infrastructure: Elliott cryogenic equipment for new LNG export projects (Port Arthur, etc.)
Semiconductor advanced packaging: TSV, HBM, chiplet processes increase CMP and vacuum pump consumption per wafer
Water treatment and environmental: Growing global demand for waste treatment O&M
Japan infrastructure renewal: Aging pump infrastructure in municipalities driving replacement orders
_externalRelationships.notes: Ebara is a diversified industrial leader with semiconductor equipment (Precision Machinery) as its highest-margin growth engine. The TSMC relationship is the most publicly confirmed and most strategic — nine consecutive supplier excellence awards is the strongest external validation of product quality. The Elliott Group turbomachinery business (US-based, majority acquired 2015) makes Ebara a global player in energy infrastructure as well. Strong foreign institutional ownership (48.56%) including activist Ichigo Trust (~10%) signals governance scrutiny and potential for further shareholder returns. FY2024 special dividend (¥115/share post-split) is an example of shareholder-friendly actions.

_metadata.dataSource: Company IR (ebara.com), Kabutan (6361), IRBank (E01542), StockAnalysis (TYO:6361), MarketScreener, Ullet shareholder data, TSMC supplier award news (Investing.com), Turbomachinery Magazine (SATORP/Elliott contract), World Pumps (Port Arthur LNG), Diamond.jp (TSMC relationship), CADDi case study (supply chain), Ebara FY2025 earnings release (Feb 13 2026)
_metadata.notes: Deep research update 2026-02-23. FY2025 revenue ¥958.3B (+10.6%), OP ¥113.8B (+16.2%), net income ¥76.6B, EPS ¥166.31. Six consecutive record profit years. FY2026 guidance: first ¥1T revenue. Stock: ¥5,638 (+112% 1yr). PER 33.9x (trailing), PBR 5.06x. Foreign ownership 48.56% (highest among covered companies). TSMC awarded Ebara CMP excellence 9+ times. Elliott >50% global share in ethylene compressors. China is largest overseas market (~22%); grew +44% YoY for Precision Machinery. Added semiconductor fab clients: CMP-0014 (Kioxia), CMP-0066 (SMIC, pre-sanctions), CMP-0093 (YMTC, pre-sanctions).
